source_pr.title: test: stablize read lock check case
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Solving #15089 

### What is changed and how it works?

Removing time duration assertion

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test


Code changes



Side effects


Related changes


Release note

symptoms: PR title: stablize read lock check case.
failure_signature: PR title: stablize read lock check case.
root_cause_explanation: The test was timing-sensitive around pessimistic locking/commit. It relied on short sleeps and a strict wall-clock duration expectation, which can intermittently fail under CI load. This PR removes the brittle elapsed-time assertion, increases lock TTL / lock-wait timeout, and adjusts waits to make the behavior more stable.
fix_pattern: Remove strict wall-clock duration assertion; widen lock TTL / lock-wait timeout and adjust waits to reduce timing sensitivity.

evidence: evidence: PR title: stablize read lock check case.
evidence: diff increases lock TTL and lock wait timeout and uses failpoint txnNotFoundRetTTL to stabilize the read lock test (session/pessimistic_test.go).
